steps.title: Find the common factor
steps.content: Both terms in $6x^2-72xy^2$ share a greatest common factor of $6x$. Factoring out that common part leaves the expression inside the parentheses and keeps the product equivalent to the original expression.

steps.title: Divide each term
steps.content: Divide each original term by $6x$ to find the remaining terms: $$ \frac{6x^2}{6x}=x $$ $$ \frac{-72xy^2}{6x}=-12y^2 $$ So the factored form is $$ 6x^2-72xy^2=6x(x-12y^2) $$ The answer is A.
